briefs.title: Proposed: Council to appoint Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for 2026 Burnaby election
briefs.summary: Council will vote on appointing Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for the City of Burnaby's 2026 General Local Election, scheduled for Saturday, October 17, 2026. The appointment is needed because the previously appointed officer is on leave and not expected to return before the election.
briefs.source_body: 7.1 APPOINTMENT OF CHIEF ELECTION OFFICER 1. CEO Appointment 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To recommend the appointment of the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election. CEO Appointment 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: Proposed: North Road BIA renewal bylaw heads to council vote
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would renew the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) for another five years. A BIA is a zone where local businesses pay a special levy (a fee on top of regular taxes) to fund marketing, events, and improvements for the area. A petition was submitted against the renewal, but city staff have confirmed the petition did not get enough valid signatures to stop the process. Council will now consider whether to approve the renewal bylaw.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, your BIA levy will continue for another five years.
briefs.source_body: 7.2 CERTIFICATE OF SUFFICIENCY: 2026 BURNABY NORTH ROAD BIA RENEWAL 1. Certificate of Sufficiency North Road BIA Renewal 2026-04-14 FINAL (sufficient)_.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Certificate of Sufficiency (North Road BIA) 2026-04-14 .pdf Purpose: To provide results for the petitions submitted against the proposed North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) renewal bylaw for a 5-year period. see Bylaw #14797 Certificate of Sufficiency North Road BIA Renewal 2026-04-14 FINAL (sufficient)_ Attachment 1 - Certificate of Sufficiency (North Road BIA) 2026-04-14 .pdf

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority 2026 corporate maintenance: KPMG auditor, Dec 12 reference date
briefs.summary: Council will vote on approving routine annual shareholder matters for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) for 2026, including: selecting December 12, 2026 as the annual reference date; appointing KPMG as auditor until the next AGM; receiving the BHA's audited financial statements for the period ending December 31, 2025; and appointing five independent directors for two-year terms and three city directors plus one independent director for three-year terms. These are administrative decisions to maintain the corporation's legal standing.
briefs.source_body: 7.3 BHA - SHAREHOLDER'S CORPORATE MAINTENANCE MATTERS FOR 2026 1. BHA - Shareholder's Corporate Maintenance Matters for 2026 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To obtain Council approval with respect to certain annual shareholder’s corporate maintenance matters for 2026 on behalf of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA). BHA - Shareholder's Corporate Maintenance Matters for 2026 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: BHA 2025 audited financials and 2026 budget — $2M operating funding contingent on Council receipt
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will review the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) 2025 audited financial statements (clean opinion from KPMG) and its 2026 operating budget. Receipt of these documents satisfies conditions for the City to provide $2.00 million in 2026 operating funding under Partnering Agreement No. 1, supporting BHA's initial housing projects at 10th Avenue and Newcombe Street and Marine Drive (Edmonds). The BHA reported an accumulated surplus of $7.52 million and cash holdings of $31.75 million as of December 31, 2025.

key_dates.label: Council meeting to consider BHA financials and budget

briefs.title: Burnaby e-bike share and e-scooter pilot bylaw amendments proposed
briefs.summary: Council will consider amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw, Parks Regulation Bylaw, and Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to allow an e-bike share pilot program and address the provincial electric kick scooter pilot program. Key proposed rules: shared e-bikes and e-scooters may only park in designated Shared Micromobility Parking Zones; no person may ride a cycle, motor assisted cycle, or electric kick scooter on a sidewalk unless permitted by a traffic control device; electric kick scooters are banned on major road networks and arterial roads except in protected cycle lanes or multi-use pathways; and no headphones over both ears while riding on a street. Violations of e-bike and e-scooter operation are moving vehicle offences enforced by RCMP under the Motor Vehicle Act.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and public hearing notice — you may want to comment if you operate a business affected by e-bike or e-scooter parking or use.
briefs.source_body: 7.5 PROPOSED BYLAW AMENDMENTS TO ADDRESS E-BIKE SHARE PILOT PROGRAM AND PROVINCIAL ELECTRIC KICK SCOOTER PILOT PROGRAM 1. Prop. Bylaw Amendments for E-Bike Share and Prov. Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Programs - 2026-04-14 .pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Proposed Bylaw Amendments (E-Bike Share and Electric Kick Scooter) 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To propose amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw and the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw to implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program within the City. briefs.title: Burnaby must submit 6-month housing target progress report by April 14, 2026
briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing submission of Burnaby's Housing Target Progress Report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs, as required by the Housing Supply Act. The report covers the first 6-month reporting period (Sept 1, 2025 – Feb 28, 2026), during which 910 net new housing units were completed (1,097 completions minus 187 demolitions). The city must meet a cumulative target of 1,536 net new units by end of Year 1 (Aug 31, 2026), with a 5-year target of 10,240 units by Aug 31, 2030. The report must be received by council resolution within 45 days of each reporting period end and submitted to Housing.Targets@gov.bc.ca.
briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time; this is a council authorization step for a mandated provincial report.

key_dates.label: Year 1a progress report submission deadline – Burnaby Housing Target Order
Year 1b reporting period ends – Burnaby Housing Target Order
Year 2 reporting period ends – Burnaby Housing Target Order
Year 3 reporting period ends – Burnaby Housing Target Order
Year 4 reporting period ends – Burnaby Housing Target Order
Year 5 final target deadline – Burnaby Housing Target Order

briefs.title: Burnaby to join Building Benchmark BC Year 7 — 30 civic buildings enrolled
briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing the City of Burnaby to participate in Year 7 of the Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) program, a voluntary provincial program that tracks and reports energy use in large buildings. The city currently includes 30 civic buildings in the program. If approved, the city would continue sharing data on its own buildings to help identify ways to save energy and reduce costs. The decision is not final — council will vote on this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners at this time — this applies to city-owned buildings only.

briefs.title: Proposed: Grant FortisBC right-of-way for gas works on Burnaby Mountain
briefs.summary: Council will consider granting a statutory right-of-way (a legal permission to use a specific piece of land) to FortisBC Energy Inc. over parts of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way. This would allow FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas pipes and equipment on that land. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.

Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 to 4 (7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way) 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To seek Council approval to grant Statutory Right-of-Way in favour of FortisBC Energy Inc. briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.35M modular four-plex pilot at 8315 14th Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on awarding a $1,354,463 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular four-plex at 8315 14th Avenue. The three-storey, 5,319 sq ft building will include four residential units (one accessible) with private outdoor space. The pilot, supported by $150,000 from Fortis BC and $200,000 from the National Research Council, tests modular construction for affordable housing. Construction is expected to take 6–8 months, with a 50–60 year useful life.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.4M contract for Jerry Rogers Creek culvert replacement
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on awarding a $2,404,076 contract to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd. for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project. The work includes installing 45 lineal metres of 1.8m x 1.8m box culvert, 40 lineal metres of DI watermain, and replacing a Metro Vancouver sanitary main. Funding comes from the Canada Community Building Fund Reserve and Development Cost Charge Drainage Reserve Fund.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.36M Central Valley Greenway culvert and paving contract
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,360,391 contract to Drake Excavating (2016) Ltd. for the Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and Paving Project. The project includes replacing 16 culverts between Sperling Avenue and Douglas Road, including major replacements at Beecher Creek and Crab Apple Creek, plus final paving of the trail. Work is funded through the 2026-2030 Financial Plan with TransLink funding for the roads component.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, plan for possible trail closures or detours during construction.

key_dates.label: Council vote on Central Valley Greenway contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on $450,000 contract change for Rogers cellular services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on approving a $450,000 contract change (including taxes) with Rogers Communications Canada Inc. to continue cellular voice and data services on a month-to-month basis for up to 9 months while the city transitions to a new provider. The total estimated contract value including this change is $3,495,900. Final payment will be based on actual services delivered.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $579,600 contract change for HVAC maintenance at city buildings
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on a contract change of $579,600 (including tax) to Just Mechanical Ltd. for HVAC system maintenance and repair services at various city-owned buildings. The change covers an interim period while the City goes back to market for competitive pricing. The total estimated contract value including this change is $5,412,750.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $394,942 three-year Cisco WebEx Contact Centre licence renewal
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposal to renew the city's Cisco WebEx Contact Center licence subscription with Long View Systems Corporation for three years (May 5, 2026 to May 4, 2029) at an estimated cost of $394,942 including taxes. The renewal covers phone-based resident assistance and IT staff support, and includes future call queue migrations.

briefs.title: Burnaby council to vote on $1.67M fire protection contract renewal
briefs.summary: Council will vote on a three-year contract renewal for A.W Fireguard Supplies (1991) Ltd. to provide fire protection system installation, maintenance, and repair services at city sites. The estimated total cost is $1,666,061 including taxes, covering July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2029. The original contract included optional renewal years, so no new bidding is required.

briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Overpass plaque wording approved for April 26 opening
briefs.summary: Council approved the commemorative plaque wording for the Burnaby Lake Overpass, which officially opens April 26, 2026. The plaque lists the mayor, councillors, and project partners (TransLink, Government of Canada, Province of BC). This is a routine approval step for a public infrastructure project — it does not change any rules or fees for businesses.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Lake Overpass grand opening and public event

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to hire external reviewer of city finances
briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to hire an outside expert to review the City of Burnaby's financial practices. The goal is to protect city assets, prevent large tax increases, and keep service costs from rising for businesses and residents. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect future tax and service costs for your business.
briefs.source_body: 8.1 FMC - EXTERNAL REVIEW OF CITY FINANCIAL PRACTICES 1. FMC - External Review of City Financial Practices 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To seek Council approval to engage an external reviewer to help safeguard the City's assets and financial standing, and to mitigate the risk of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents. FMC - External Review of City Financial Practices 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: Proposed: 20-storey, 215-unit rental rezoning at 5035 Beresford Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider forwarding a rezoning application (REZ #25-11) for a proposed 20-storey apartment building with 215 market rental units (32 studio, 98 one-bedroom, 65 two-bedroom, 20 three-bedroom) at 5035 Beresford Street. The project includes 574.74 m² outdoor and 646.16 m² indoor amenity space, 38 vehicle parking spaces (100% EV-ready), 441 secured bicycle spaces, and 1 loading space. A minimum of 20% of units will be adaptable. This is the first step — if council votes to forward it, the application will go to First and Second Reading at a future meeting. No final decision has been made yet.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to have a say on the proposed rezoning.

briefs.title: Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth: final recommendations due June 23, 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council received an interim update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience. The task force has identified strategic priorities and is validating them through surveys and 10 stakeholder interviews conducted March 13–April 2, 2026. It will reconvene April 16, 2026 for three additional sessions through end of May to develop actionable recommendations, which will be presented to Council on June 23, 2026. No new rules or decisions have been proposed yet.

key_dates.label: Task Force reconvenes for final sessions

briefs.source_body: 9.1 UPDATE ON MAYOR'S TASK FORCE ON BURNABY ECONOMIC GROWTH AND RESILIENCE 1. Update on Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To update members of Council on the progress of the Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience. Update on Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience 2026-04-1

briefs.title: Developer forfeits $1,363,500 security for public pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court
briefs.summary: Council received a report that Oxford Properties forfeited a $1,363,500 security deposit instead of building the public amenity pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court, part of the Riverbend Business Park rezoning. The City now holds the funds and may use them for the pier or an alternative amenity along the Fraser Foreshore or elsewhere in Burnaby. The covenant will be discharged from title once the forfeited security is received.
briefs.action_required: No immediate action required — watch for future Council direction on how the forfeited $1,363,500 will be used.
briefs.source_body: 9.2 AMENITY PIER COVENANT - DEVELOPER ELECTION TO FORFEIT SECURITY 1. Amenity Pier Covenant Developer Election to Forfeit Security 2026-04-14.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 and 2 (Amenity Pier Covenant) 2026-04-14.pdf Purpose: To provide Council with information regarding the owner’s election under a Section 219 Covenant registered on title at 8351 Fraser Reach Court to forfeit the posted security and not proceed with construction of the public amenity pier contemplated under the Riverbend Business Park rezoning approvals, and the City’s holding of the forfeited security pending future Council direction. Amenity Pier Covenant Developer Election to Forfeit Security 2026-04-14.pdf Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 and 2 (Amenity Pier Covenant) 2026-04-14.pdf

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw up for final adoption — 5-year levy
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of Bylaw #14797, which would create a new Business Improvement Area (BIA) along Burnaby North Road. The BIA would run from April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2031. Properties classified as Class 5 (light industry) or Class 6 (business/other) within the designated area would pay a local service tax based on assessed value to fund a total grant of $1,755,147.81 over five years ($317,954.94 in Year 1). Enough property owners have submitted formal petitions against the proposal, so the outcome is uncertain.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if the BIA is approved, your property taxes could increase if you own Class 5 or Class 6 property in the designated area.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA term begins (if adopted)
Burnaby North Road BIA term ends
